name: Index-Serving PR Review Workflow
description: Scan for index-serving PR review requests, fan out per-PR review agents, and join the review artifacts.

input.title: Scan index-serving PR review requests
input.prompt:
Use the Slack tools to scan recent Slack messages available to me across my workspace from the start of today in my local timezone up to now.

Read relevant messages and thread replies. Find every GitHub pull request mentioned in those messages that requires my attention to review. A PR requires my attention if the message or thread tags me directly, mentions my name, asks me to review, asks for my approval, or otherwise implies I am the intended reviewer.

My Slack display name or user ID is: U09F7PGF4AJ.

Keep only PRs that belong to the index-serving repository.

Write a JSON object with this shape:
{
  "prs": [
    {
      "number": 123,
      "title": "PR title",
      "url": "https://github.com/<owner>/index-serving/pull/123",
      "author": "author if known",
      "channelName": "channel if known",
      "slackPermalink": "message permalink if known",
      "reason": "why this needs my review",
      "threadStatus": "notable status from the thread if any"
    }
  ]
}

If no index-serving PRs need review, write {"prs":[]}.
Do not modify source code.

input.title: Review index-serving PR #{{item.number}}
